Japan’s benchmark index slid more than 4%, weighed down by a 13% plunge in SoftBank Group’s shares, after a media report suggested OpenAI could hold off going public until next year. items.title: Dow Finally Tops 52,000 With Some Help From Its Newest Member
items.summary: 다우지수는 새로운 회원이 빅테크 및 칩 주식의 랠리에 합류한 날 마침내 52,000위로 마감했습니다. 이번 주 다우지수에서 Verizon을 대체한 Alphabet이 지수를 주도했습니다. Mizuho의 Daniel O'Regan은 "이번 달은 매우 변동성이 컸습니다. 일부 변동은 완전히 역사적이었고 지금은 솔직히 약간 불안합니다"라고 썼습니다.
items.summaryOriginal: The Dow finally closed above the 52,000 the same day its newest member joined a rally in Big Tech and chip stocks. Alphabet, which replaced Verizon in the Dow this week, led the index. “This month has been VERY volatile — some of the swings have been outright historic, and in the moment, honestly a bit unsettling,” writes Mizuho’s Daniel O’Regan.
